transcript.text: Hey there, how's it going Garth?
Great, I'm just someone a little long-winded, but thanks for reaching out.
I was pretty excited about what I saw on the website, but sometimes you send out these messages on websites and they kind of go into the ether.
So I guess for you guys, that would be sort of counter to what you do.
So I'm glad to see it works.
Yeah, for sure, for sure.
Well, great to connect.
Obviously, we're a bit limited on time, so we'd love to hear a bit what was on your mind and happy to share whatever I can to see if there's a good fit.
And if it makes sense to go deeper, we can find time to go deeper.
So I'll do a quick on me so you get a sense of who the hell I even am.
So I'm VP of customer.
For growth over here, worn many different hats, helping lead on the sales side.
In a few weeks, I'll probably be doing other things, know, classic startup stuff.
I was leading the client services side of the business for most of my tenure here.
And a little over a year ago, actually, I was the first customer.
So I've really seen like whole angles and sides of the journey here.
I'm not a typical sales guy.
I am more of a...
of What were you doing before?
Yeah, was head of growth at Homebase.
So that's where a lot of my background was.
So previous to GrowthX, spent 19 years.
Basically, brands would hire me because they've typically been too reliant on paid search.
They need to diversify their channels.
And, you know, they bring me on to help them figure that out.
Usually organic is a big part of that mix, especially the past few years.
It's a little bit different now, but when capital was a little bit harder to get.
And CAC was all of a sudden being paid attention to a whole lot more.
Organic, you know, just got the light showing on it quite a bit.
A lot of my experiences there, that's what led me to sign up.
I was driving a bunch of growth.
Homebase was pretty excited, but I had to rethink things with my budget because I was quite confident it was going to get slashed last year and met Marcel at the perfect time.
He was doing some workshops and sharing what he was building when he was CMO at DeepGram.
The rest was history.

You know, we started doing a lot of experimentation around AI content, finding a lot of people wanted the intent, you know, support and strategy of help build the workflows, the strategy, execute the content, help me just like operationalize all this.

And so that's really what we've been leaning into and building and have been able to validate with a whole lot of brands now, varying stages early, you know, first content live on the site to public companies that you can drive growth with the right strategy with AI content.
And I've done a lot of experimentation, you know, a lot of like, what are those right people?
Like, how do you need to evolve these workflows?
What are the inputs to make this, you know, repeatable in terms of outcomes?
So, you know, happy to share if it makes sense to go into the weeds on all that, but that's a bit of a high level.
So a lot of brands come to us, usually, you know, they have some sort of need around scaling organic demand.
You know, usually that starts with helping generate traffic first, but at the end of the day, if we're just driving traffic, that's usually not going to keep our customers satisfied.
So it's about, like, where do we start that journey to engaging the right people, being able to think about different ways to engage them.
And that can mean a whole lot of different things, typical mediums, et cetera.
But a lot of times, our specialty lies on, like, helping first produce content for your website that's highly targeted to the people you want to talk to.
And then over time, how do we create demand from that?
A lot of us are former in-house marketers, right?
And so we've built these programs in a whole variety of ways.
And we just find that a lot of our customers need a lot of that, like, foundational support.
So that's a lot where we come in and, you know, a bit of our superpower is just how fast we can go.

So, you know, that's a benefit of AI, but there's a bit of a, a lot of things we've learned of, like, when are you ready to hit the gas and how can be confident that you're hitting the gas on the right things?
So that's a lot of, like, how we think and how we refine our approach here.
All right.
That makes sense.
So I am, so I am, I've spent the last several years as mostly an investor and then a tech, like, venture-backed company CFO.
Recently, I've grown really, really tired of that work.

And because of that, I have, I've started to spend a little bit of time recently doing some consulting on that side, but also going back and helping my, my mother look at coming up with a succession plan for her business, which she started 25 years ago, just like a behavioral health practice in, in right outside of DC.
And for me, I'm,
So that, you know, your kid, your parents, an entrepreneur, you kind of like just grow up in the business and you get to know all the ups and downs and stuff.
And I always kind of wanted to stay as far as far away as I possibly could from this industry and business because of how challenging it has been through the years for her.
A couple of things have changed recently that I think have changed.
And basically, we've gone from two things.

We've gone from basically the destigmatization phase of behavioral health treatment to now folks really understand not only that it's important for overall health, but that, you know, actually having integrating behavioral health treatment into like with your regular sort of primary care actually lowers overall expenses associated with health care.
So there's a, there's a big push to push behavioral health.
there's a, there's push Thank
Into pretty much every care setting, which is a big change.
When my mom was doing it, was just like, how do we make people not embarrassed to say they went to therapy?
And the other part is a lot of the technology that you were talking about.

I don't know how familiar you are with this, but there's a big, a lot of the growth in healthcare expenses has been around the administrative expenses for doing things like working with commercial insurances and all the compliance.

And, you know, phone calls and scheduling and all that fun stuff, which for me, just being a little bit closer to the technology stuff, like I know that even if we are not there right now, which we may be, a lot of those sort of operational and administrative responsibilities are like, we're probably getting pretty close to being able to automate a lot of that, which means that we can increase the impact that we have in the community.
And,
Do it in a sort of financially sustainable and scalable, high quality way that has been really challenging to do with manual processes in the past.
So I'm looking at ways for us to do, for us to implement that new technology all over the place, operations, clinical operations, but also in marketing.
We have a little bit of a complicated thing because we do government contracting and we do, we do like obviously patient care.
So we have sort of three categories of types of audience that we're trying to hit is like one is the actual patients to say, hey, look, I want to have a problem that I want to work through.
I want to find a therapist or I want to find I want to have an evaluation done.
How do I go find this company to do that?
The second are sort of referral sources like, you know, hospital systems and primary care doctors that would benefit from integrating behavioral health into what they do.
And the third are sort of government contract, government contractors who are looking for healthcare staffing solutions or clinical solutions.
And we've done it in all kinds of different settings like prisons and schools and military bases.
And so with that, like with that sort of varied a group of audiences and on a sort of a small business approach, we've got three folks in marketing.
And when I ask them, like, what exactly they're responsible for, it's like a little bit of everything.
Right.
And if we had like lists, like if, you know, some folks are saying, hey, look, I make calls to primary care doctors.
And I'm like, OK, what's the pitch?
And it's like, I don't know.
And it's like, well, what's the primary care docs we're reaching out to?
And it's like, well, I kind of go Google stuff and figure it out as I go along.
And for me, I'm like, I could probably get some value out of those folks at some point.

So in the short term, I feel as though I need to at least build some like structure around what we're doing, like who we're going after, how we're going after them and what we're saying when we get to them.
And I've been a little bit overwhelmed by the number of tools out there that do like parts of it.
So when I saw, I mean, you must, right?

Like when I saw with you guys, at least how I interpreted what I saw on the website, I was like, okay, maybe like they can, this is a way for me to get like the best of the tools and like the outcomes I'm looking for without feeling the need to have to like build a bunch of like my own go-to-market AI stacked and everything.
And because I don't know how realistic that is for me in the short term.
Yeah, totally.
That makes sense.
I think I follow, certainly have heard similar things before.

Some of what you mentioned, no doubt there are things within our approach where I think we could provide a lot more foundation building and clarity over who you're speaking to, what we're learning about that, etc.

I think the key would be, what would be the dynamics, you know, kind of like what is the menu of things that you kind of need to have covered and how much of that would we actually realistically be able to support.
Because generally, like where we like to start our engagements is more on what we call this like kind of like editorial layer where we're building that strategy for you.
And we found that it's a good place to start because there's so much coordination that goes into getting that right.
Some of what you mentioned, for example, like, you know, your tone, voice, just all the different things and really, really dialing that in.
We put a ton of effort into really diving deep into that.
We find that when you're able to create these different.

And start to feed them into AI workflows, it tends to produce more repeatable results, but also has the dual benefit of putting in a format where you can give us feedback and we can have that back and forth of what's actually hitting the right notes, helping continue to educate our team about who we're speaking to, et cetera.
And so that's somewhat like the upfront strategic prep work to being able to scale and build a content strategy.
And then from there, it's like, okay, what topic should we go after?
Let's really dive in deep and start to have that back and forth and start to attack this from multiple angles.

The way we like to think about it is like, oh, the people you want to speak to, like, you know, considering things like applying things like jobs to be done frameworks or other competitor analyses or what we can see in social, et cetera.
There's a million different ways this kind of skin, this capital, like, what are those key questions that are being asked this audience and how can we create content?
That's the most amazing answers to that.
And sometimes that can be a combination of like, what does that experience look and feel?
But also what are those words on the page?
And really starting to dial that in and start to scale the roadmap from there.
So I can see how that would check some of the boxes.

But sometimes I have these conversations, and I don't know if this is applicable here, but I tend to lean on, hey, it sounds like you might need a growth generalist first to lay some foundation for you.
And then we can help come in and help kind of build on top of that.
Like, for example, the way I think about those sometimes, and maybe you already have this being presumptuous just based off of some of the things you shared.
But like, hey, we can send more eyeballs to your site.
We can do all the different things to ensure it's the right people and what should happen afterwards and provide some recommendations.
But if you don't have some of the necessary elements of that engine to handling that inbound, and what happens next, and who do they go to, and how does that process work?
Like, a lot of us have done that in varying ways.
It's just not necessarily part of, like, the core offer.

We typically do here, and maybe in the future, we add that into the mix, but just naturally being a startup and trying to not spread ourselves too thin and focus on what we have high degree of confidence we can execute and deliver repeatable outcomes on, we've kind of focused first on that kind of content editorial layer.
And oftentimes, as we do and we build that, we find paths for further distribution, things we can experiment with.
We've certainly done things that exist outside of that because a lot of our methodology can be applied to like sales enablement to, you know, a bunch of different things.
We just tend to be quite selective with that because, you know, it is a bit of a, you know, we're a bit of a hybrid, right?

We have the platform side of the business that has largely been focused on enabling our team because we found most folks wanted that kind of, to your point, they didn't want to buy a bunch of different tools and they needed help of like really thinking through the strategy and, and so really anything we think of is custom.
have to balance of like, that takes time away from us building our own platform as well.

And so, so anyways, being a little presumptuous based off time and things of that nature, and maybe I'm off on some of that, but that's generally when I talk to folks similarly as yourself and kind of part of what you mentioned, usually my recommendation is like, I want to like oversell you on something that's like, you're not necessarily ready for, but maybe you are more than I appreciate.
And so happy to dig in further, but that's just like initial thoughts.
We're not ready for it, but how, like, how would I, like, let's say I, let's say that I, I am like the, I am one of these folks, like I'm a finance guy, right?
So like, I get, I get best sold by not being sold, right?
So now, now I'm like super intrigued, right?

And I, I'd say, I'd say like, if I wanted to know what are the things that we need to have in place to like be sort of a better fit, like that would actually, you you know, selfishly, that would actually probably help me as.
Because again, a finance guy, not a marketer, like it would actually help me put together sort of the marketing development roadmap that I need to get in place over time before going to you.
Do you have a sense of what that is or like how you guide folks who maybe you have these types of conversations with?
Generally, you know, I don't have something that's like super like packageable I can provide.
So there's a couple of things.
One would be generally it is around like, okay, what happens next?
Like, you know, are they booking some sort of consultation?
How is that ingested?
Who does that go to?
How do we measure where that came from?
And maybe you already have some of this.
I'm just, you know, throwing things that are boxes I would want to have checked, right?
Because at the end of the day, as we start to increase what we do for folks like yourself, we need to have some sort of like methodology to like come back.
Is this actually working?
Like, can
We actually measure and give you, because you're going to be paying for a service and you're going to say, am I getting a good return on these dollars?
And so we need to ensure that, like, we address the friction to ultimately them getting to the outcome we want them to get to.
In this case, I would assume it's some sort of booking a consultation or something.
To that effect, I could be wrong.
And, like, how do we measure that?
Yeah, what happens next, et cetera.
And so, and then from there, you know, it tends to be other things in terms of, this is a little bit different.
We tend to work with a lot of SaaS companies, but so, you know, maybe less applicable here.
But, you know, we tend to think about things around product market fit.
And so I think maybe there is an aspect of that to some degree here to think about of, like, you know, who are those right folks that you want to be talking about and what is the next step?
But, you know, we have some cases like we work with, probably the closest to what you, quite different.

What you were sharing, they do ketamine there across the U.S., so, you know, for us, for them, it is a lot of, like, how do we scale, like, the practitioners and, like, really get more traction in the markets that they can support.

So we do a variety of different things around editorial, but also, like, programmatic opportunities of, like, when people are going after, like, X city plus this type of searches, like, how are we improving their visibility?

And then they have, like, a whole system that, like, they can track the booking of consultations, and then we can get that data back and understand, like, are we actually, like, getting traction on the audience that, you know, they care about?
So, the other thing I can offer, I'm happy to send this out after the call, as we are doing a workshop next week on, first, it's free.
People tend to go nuts for these, like, we provide a ton of value and depth and get a ton of amazing feedback after these, and it is going to get.
It's in the weeds, but, like, really, it is sharing a lot of, like, what we've learned the past year and doing a lot of experimentation, you know, building these workflows, creating this content.
And so you'll get a bit of, like, a deeper picture if you, you know, happy to also send the recording.
But that could be there.
You need to get your head around, you know, how we think and where that could fit in with you all, et cetera.

And then, you know, if that seems interesting and maybe we're closer, happy to, you know, pick up the conversation after that and see if, you know, with more specificity, like, what would it look like to partner?
That's perfect.
Cool.
All right.
I'll join that and I will follow up on it after and see, you know, how far off we are.
But, again, man, like, I really do appreciate you making the time.
I'm sorry I was late.
And I also just, again, like, for maybe it's a tip you sold to Finance Pulse before, but, you know, it's the anti-sell that really, really works.
So that it's not intentional, but I have noticed the pattern at times, you know, at the end of the day, I just.
I want to make sure, you know, it's a good partnership and, you know, we work off a lot of referral-based business.
And so, you know, keeping that in mind, it's worked pretty well for us.
All right.
Well, look, I'll join next week and I'll be, I'll get back to you.
I'll keep an eye out for the email about it.
Yeah, of course.
Yeah, I'll send that in just a bit.
Great meeting you, Garth.
And yeah, feel free to reach out to any questions in the meantime.
Absolutely.
Well, thanks for the time.
All right.
You as well.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Introductory call between GrowthX and Interdynamics to explore potential collaboration on AI-driven content marketing strategies.](https://fathom.video/share/6tXEpRekW7d62NU-93ssyhQSzxVyWYLa?tab=summary&timestamp=2.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [GrowthX specializes in AI-powered content creation and organic growth strategies, with a focus on building foundational editorial layers.](https://fathom.video/share/6tXEpRekW7d62NU-93ssyhQSzxVyWYLa?tab=summary&timestamp=600.0)
  - [Interdynamics, a behavioral health practice, seeks marketing support for multiple audience segments (patients, referral sources, government contractors).](https://fathom.video/share/6tXEpRekW7d62NU-93ssyhQSzxVyWYLa?tab=summary&timestamp=442.0)
  - [GrowthX's approach may not fully align with Interdynamics' immediate needs; a growth generalist might be beneficial first.](https://fathom.video/share/6tXEpRekW7d62NU-93ssyhQSzxVyWYLa?tab=summary&timestamp=728.0)
  - [Kyle invited Garth to an upcoming GrowthX workshop to learn more about their methodologies and AI content creation workflows.](https://fathom.video/share/6tXEpRekW7d62NU-93ssyhQSzxVyWYLa?tab=summary&timestamp=1063.0)

## Topics

### GrowthX's Background and Expertise

  - [Kyle: VP of Customer, former head of growth at Homebase](https://fathom.video/share/6tXEpRekW7d62NU-93ssyhQSzxVyWYLa?tab=summary&timestamp=58.0)
  - [19 years experience helping brands diversify from paid search, esp. to organic](https://fathom.video/share/6tXEpRekW7d62NU-93ssyhQSzxVyWYLa?tab=summary&timestamp=92.0)
  - [Specializes in AI content creation, intent support, strategy building](https://fathom.video/share/6tXEpRekW7d62NU-93ssyhQSzxVyWYLa?tab=summary&timestamp=141.0)
  - [Focus on scaling organic demand, starting with traffic generation](https://fathom.video/share/6tXEpRekW7d62NU-93ssyhQSzxVyWYLa?tab=summary&timestamp=186.0)
  - [Emphasis on foundational support and rapid execution](https://fathom.video/share/6tXEpRekW7d62NU-93ssyhQSzxVyWYLa?tab=summary&timestamp=233.0)

### Interdynamics' Business and Challenges

  - [25-year-old behavioral health practice outside DC](https://fathom.video/share/6tXEpRekW7d62NU-93ssyhQSzxVyWYLa?tab=summary&timestamp=275.0)
  - [Recent industry shifts: destigmatization, integration with primary care](https://fathom.video/share/6tXEpRekW7d62NU-93ssyhQSzxVyWYLa?tab=summary&timestamp=321.0)
  - [Three key audience segments: patients, referral sources, government contractors](https://fathom.video/share/6tXEpRekW7d62NU-93ssyhQSzxVyWYLa?tab=summary&timestamp=442.0)
  - [Current marketing team (3 people) lacks clear structure and strategies](https://fathom.video/share/6tXEpRekW7d62NU-93ssyhQSzxVyWYLa?tab=summary&timestamp=511.0)
  - [Seeking tools and support to build go-to-market AI stack](https://fathom.video/share/6tXEpRekW7d62NU-93ssyhQSzxVyWYLa?tab=summary&timestamp=552.0)

### GrowthX's Approach and Potential Fit

  - [Starts with "editorial layer" to build strategy and tone/voice](https://fathom.video/share/6tXEpRekW7d62NU-93ssyhQSzxVyWYLa?tab=summary&timestamp=638.0)
  - [Uses AI workflows for repeatable results](https://fathom.video/share/6tXEpRekW7d62NU-93ssyhQSzxVyWYLa?tab=summary&timestamp=661.0)
  - [Applies frameworks like jobs-to-be-done, competitor analysis](https://fathom.video/share/6tXEpRekW7d62NU-93ssyhQSzxVyWYLa?tab=summary&timestamp=700.0)
  - [Creates content answering key audience questions](https://fathom.video/share/6tXEpRekW7d62NU-93ssyhQSzxVyWYLa?tab=summary&timestamp=714.0)
  - [May not cover all Interdynamics' immediate needs (e.g., inbound handling, process workflows)](https://fathom.video/share/6tXEpRekW7d62NU-93ssyhQSzxVyWYLa?tab=summary&timestamp=763.0)

### Readiness Assessment for GrowthX Services

  - [Need for clear next steps after content engagement (e.g., consultation booking)](https://fathom.video/share/6tXEpRekW7d62NU-93ssyhQSzxVyWYLa?tab=summary&timestamp=925.0)
  - [Importance of measurement and attribution](https://fathom.video/share/6tXEpRekW7d62NU-93ssyhQSzxVyWYLa?tab=summary&timestamp=960.0)
  - [Consideration of product-market fit (even for service businesses)](https://fathom.video/share/6tXEpRekW7d62NU-93ssyhQSzxVyWYLa?tab=summary&timestamp=1000.0)
  - [Example: GrowthX's work with ketamine clinics (editorial + programmatic strategies)](https://fathom.video/share/6tXEpRekW7d62NU-93ssyhQSzxVyWYLa?tab=summary&timestamp=1020.0)

### Next Steps and Further Exploration

  - [Kyle to send invitation for upcoming GrowthX workshop](https://fathom.video/share/6tXEpRekW7d62NU-93ssyhQSzxVyWYLa?tab=summary&timestamp=1063.0)
  - [Workshop will cover learnings from past year, AI content creation workflows](https://fathom.video/share/6tXEpRekW7d62NU-93ssyhQSzxVyWYLa?tab=summary&timestamp=1080.0)
  - [Garth to attend workshop and reassess potential fit afterward](https://fathom.video/share/6tXEpRekW7d62NU-93ssyhQSzxVyWYLa?tab=summary&timestamp=1101.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Kyle to send GrowthX workshop invitation to Garth](https://fathom.video/share/6tXEpRekW7d62NU-93ssyhQSzxVyWYLa?tab=summary&timestamp=1159.0)
  - [Garth to attend the workshop and review recording](https://fathom.video/share/6tXEpRekW7d62NU-93ssyhQSzxVyWYLa?tab=summary&timestamp=1156.0)
  - [Follow-up discussion after the workshop to assess partnership potential](https://fathom.video/share/6tXEpRekW7d62NU-93ssyhQSzxVyWYLa?tab=summary&timestamp=1101.0)
  - [Garth to consider developing a marketing development roadmap based on insights from the call and workshop](https://fathom.video/share/6tXEpRekW7d62NU-93ssyhQSzxVyWYLa?tab=summary&timestamp=884.0)

action_items.description: Send workshop details/invite to Garth for next week's free AI content strategy workshop
Join GrowthX's free AI content strategy workshop next week
Follow up with Kyle after workshop to discuss potential partnership and assess readiness
